vulns.desc: Race condition (TOCTOU) in voucher redemption: POST /redeem checks voucher.used, then credits the wallet and sets used=True with NO lock between check and act. Firing concurrent requests for a single-use voucher lets all of them pass the check before any marks it used, so the voucher is redeemed multiple times and the wallet is over-credited. Only findable by sending parallel requests.
vulns.exploit: Login -> fire ~20 concurrent POST /redeem {voucher:WELCOME50} -> balance ends up a multiple of 50 instead of 50 (voucher redeemed many times).

vulns.desc: IDOR: GET /wallet?user=<name> returns ANY user's balance and profile with no authentication or ownership check, so an attacker enumerates and reads every account.
vulns.exploit: GET /wallet?user=bob (with no/own session) -> bob's balance and profile.

vulns.desc: Mass assignment: POST /profile blindly merges the whole request body into the account, so privileged fields the client should never control (balance, is_admin) are accepted and applied.
vulns.exploit: POST /profile {"balance":1000000,"is_admin":true} with your own token -> wallet balance and admin flag set directly.

vulns.desc: Lack of rate limiting / lockout on POST /login: unlimited credential attempts with no throttle, delay, or lockout enable brute force and credential stuffing.
vulns.exploit: Loop POST /login with candidate passwords -> no 429, no lockout, no delay.

vulns.desc: Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
